Author: thies <thieso@gmail.com>
Date:   Thu Feb 26 16:58:10 2026 +0100

    fix: supply chain (#2), --keep-data (#5), dockyard-stack (#6)
    
    #2 — SHA256 verification on all downloaded tarballs (docker CE,
    docker rootless extras, sysbox). verify_checksum() called for both
    fresh downloads and cache hits to prevent cache-poisoning attacks.
    
    #5 — `destroy --keep-data` flag preserves lib/docker/ (container data
    and images) while removing all runtime state. Prints data size warning
    on full destroy; prints preservation path on --keep-data.
    
    #6 — dockyard-stack wrapper script: starts sysbox-mgr, sysbox-fs,
    containerd, dockerd as children in the same systemd cgroup; sends
    sd_notify READY=1 once all daemon sockets are up; monitors daemons
    every 2s; on SIGTERM/SIGINT shuts down in reverse order. Service uses
    Type=notify + ExecStartPost to gate "systemctl start" on full docker
    API readiness. KillMode=process ensures ordered cleanup on stop.
    
    test suite: add alpine preloading from /var/tmp/alpine.tar into DinD
    containers (tests 10, 25) and nested instance (test 29) to avoid
    Docker Hub pull rate limits; add test 22 retry (90s) for cold-boot
    timing on slow KVM VMs; verify preloads from host cache too.
    
    Validated 29/29 on Ubuntu 24.04 LTS kernel 6.8 (100.106.185.92).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

1→cmd_destroy() {
     2→    local YES=false
     3→    local KEEP_DATA=false
     4→    for arg in "$@"; do
     5→        case "$arg" in
     6→            --yes|-y)       YES=true ;;
     7→            --keep-data|-k) KEEP_DATA=true ;;

    10→    done
    11→
    12→    require_root
    13→
    14→    local SERVICE_FILE="/etc/systemd/system/${SERVICE_NAME}.service"

    36→            exit 0

    66→

    69→

1→cmd_enable() {
     2→    require_root
     3→
     4→    local SERVICE_FILE="/etc/systemd/system/${SERVICE_NAME}.service"
     5→
     6→    if [ -f "$SERVICE_FILE" ]; then
     7→        echo "Error: ${SERVICE_FILE} already exists." >&2
     8→        exit 1
     9→    fi
    10→
    11→    echo "Installing ${SERVICE_NAME}.service (with per-instance sysbox)..."
    12→
    13→    # Write the stack script (bakes all paths in at install time; no env file at runtime)
    14→    cat > "${BIN_DIR}/dockyard-stack" <<STACKEOF
    15→#!/bin/bash
    16→set -euo pipefail
    17→
    18→MGR_PID=""
    19→FS_PID=""
    20→CTR_PID=""
    21→DOCKERD_PID=""
    22→
    23→wait_for_socket() {
    24→    local sock="\$1" pid="\$2" name="\$3" i=0
    25→    while [ ! -S "\$sock" ]; do
    26→        sleep 1
    27→        i=\$((i+1))
    28→        if ! kill -0 "\$pid" 2>/dev/null; then
    29→            echo "dockyard-stack: \$name exited unexpectedly" >&2
    30→            return 1
    31→        fi
    32→        if [ "\$i" -ge 30 ]; then
    33→            echo "dockyard-stack: \$name did not start within 30s" >&2
    34→            return 1
    35→        fi
    36→    done
    37→}
    38→
    39→cleanup() {
    40→    local code=\${1:-0}
    41→    for pid in "\$DOCKERD_PID" "\$CTR_PID" "\$FS_PID" "\$MGR_PID"; do
    42→        [ -z "\$pid" ] && continue
    43→        kill "\$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    44→        wait "\$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    45→    done
    46→    exit "\$code"
    47→}
    48→
    49→trap 'cleanup 0' TERM INT
    50→
    51→# --- Start sysbox-mgr ---
    52→${BIN_DIR}/sysbox-mgr --run-dir ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR} --data-root ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR} \
    53→    >>${LOG_DIR}/sysbox-mgr.log 2>&1 &
    54→MGR_PID=\$!
    55→echo "\$MGR_PID" > ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-mgr.pid
    56→wait_for_socket ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysmgr.sock "\$MGR_PID" sysbox-mgr || cleanup 1
    57→
    58→# --- Start sysbox-fs ---
    59→${BIN_DIR}/sysbox-fs --run-dir ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR} --mountpoint ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR} \
    60→    >>${LOG_DIR}/sysbox-fs.log 2>&1 &
    61→FS_PID=\$!
    62→echo "\$FS_PID" > ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysbox-fs.pid
    63→wait_for_socket ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysfs.sock "\$FS_PID" sysbox-fs || cleanup 1
    64→
    65→# --- Start containerd ---
    66→${BIN_DIR}/containerd \
    67→    --root ${DOCKER_DATA}/containerd \
    68→    --state ${RUN_DIR}/containerd \
    69→    --address ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET} \
    70→    >>${LOG_DIR}/containerd.log 2>&1 &
    71→CTR_PID=\$!
    72→echo "\$CTR_PID" > ${RUN_DIR}/containerd.pid
    73→wait_for_socket ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET} "\$CTR_PID" containerd || cleanup 1
    74→
    75→# --- Start dockerd ---
    76→${BIN_DIR}/dockerd \
    77→    --config-file ${ETC_DIR}/daemon.json \
    78→    --containerd ${CONTAINERD_SOCKET} \
    79→    --data-root ${DOCKER_DATA} \
    80→    --exec-root ${RUN_DIR} \
    81→    --pidfile ${RUN_DIR}/dockerd.pid \
    82→    --bridge ${BRIDGE} \
    83→    --fixed-cidr ${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR} \
    84→    --default-address-pool base=${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE},size=${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE} \
    85→    --host unix://${DOCKER_SOCKET} \
    86→    --iptables=false \
    87→    --group ${INSTANCE_GROUP} \
    88→    >>${LOG_DIR}/dockerd.log 2>&1 &
    89→DOCKERD_PID=\$!
    90→wait_for_socket ${DOCKER_SOCKET} "\$DOCKERD_PID" dockerd || cleanup 1
    91→
    92→# Signal systemd that all daemon sockets are up.

    94→systemd-notify --ready 2>/dev/null || true
    95→
    96→# Monitor: if any daemon dies, trigger a restart
    97→while true; do
    98→    sleep 2 &
    99→    wait \$! 2>/dev/null || true
   100→    for check in "sysbox-mgr:\$MGR_PID" "sysbox-fs:\$FS_PID" "containerd:\$CTR_PID" "dockerd:\$DOCKERD_PID"; do

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have comprehensive understanding. Let me create the summary.

## Comprehensive Dockyard Analysis Summary

### What is Dockyard

Dockyard is a **multi-instance Docker daemon manager** that runs fully isolated, independent Docker instances on a single host, each with sysbox-runc as the default runtime. It's a single-script installer/manager that solves the fundamental problem of Docker being a singleton — enabling daemon-level isolation without VMs.

**Key insight for Sandcastle integration:** Dockyard is what powers Sandcastle's per-user Docker daemon isolation. Sandcastle would instantiate Dockyard to create isolated Docker environments for each user or sandbox.

---

### Core Architecture

**Per-Instance Topology:**
```
dockyard instance (e.g., dy1_)
├── sysbox-mgr + sysbox-fs (per-instance pair, not shared)
├── containerd (per-instance)
├── dockerd (per-instance)
├── Linux bridge (e.g., dy1_docker0)
├── System user/group (dy1_docker)
├── Complete data root (bin/, etc/, lib/, run/, log/)
└── All iptables rules scoped to bridge name (isolated)
```

```

```

- Ordered shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT in reverse order
- **KillMode=process** ensures clean cleanup

#### 3. **Reliability Audit Fixes** (commits 2c87943, eb88ac8, 089c3c8)
- Fixed 8 reliability/safety issues from expert review
- Fixed trap EXIT regression (causes unbound variable errors at script exit)

- Improved error handling throughout

**Pinned Binary Versions (hardcoded in `create`):**

| Software | Version | Source | Why |
|----------|---------|--------|-----|
| Docker CE static | 29.2.1 | download.docker.com/linux/static/stable/ | Latest stable; sysbox-runc is default so bundled runc 1.3.3 is never used for sandbox containers |
| Docker Rootless Extras | 29.2.1 | download.docker.com/linux/static/stable/ | Provides vpnkit, rootlesskit (for future features) |
| Sysbox (fork) | **0.6.7.10-tc** | github.com/thieso2/sysbox | Fork adds `--run-dir` for per-instance isolation; 0.6.7.10-tc is first with aarch64 tarball |

- The fork (`thieso2/sysbox`) adds `--run-dir` flag to all three sysbox binaries (sysbox-mgr, sysbox-fs, sysbox-runc)
- sysbox-runc 0.6.7.9+ parses `--run-dir` directly from `os.Args` in `init()`, bypassing urfave/cli v1 quirks
- This is **NOT** compatible with upstream Nestybox sysbox 0.6.7 CE (hardcoded socket paths)
- **Breaking constraint:** DinD inner containers must use `docker:26.1-dind` or earlier (runc 1.1.12), not `docker:27.x-dind` (runc 1.3.x) — the newer runc has strict procfs safety checks that reject sysbox-fs's virtualised `/proc`

**Systemd Service** (`/etc/systemd/system/${PREFIX}docker.service`):
- Type: `notify` (waits for `READY=1` from dockyard-stack)
- ExecStart: `${BIN_DIR}/dockyard-stack` (monitoring script above)
- ExecStartPre: Insert 4 iptables rules (all scoped to instance bridge)
- ExecStopPost: Remove 4 iptables rules
- KillMode: `process` (kills only top-level process, letting dockyard-stack handle cleanup)
- Restart: `always` or `on-failure`

**Startup Order (dockyard-stack):**
1. sysbox-mgr starts with `--run-dir ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR} --data-root ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR}`
2. Wait for `${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysmgr.sock`
3. sysbox-fs starts with `--run-dir ${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR} --mountpoint ${SYSBOX_DATA_DIR}`
4. Wait for `${SYSBOX_RUN_DIR}/sysfs.sock`
5. containerd starts
6. Wait for `${CONTAINERD_SOCKET}`
7. dockerd starts with `--default-runtime sysbox-runc --group ${INSTANCE_GROUP} --iptables=false`
8. Wait for `${DOCKER_SOCKET}`
9. Send `sd_notify READY=1` to systemd

**Shutdown Order (on SIGTERM):**
- dockerd → containerd → sysbox-fs → sysbox-mgr
- All cleanups are idempotent

### Networking Model

**Explicit iptables (not Docker-managed):**
Each instance injects exactly **4 rules** on startup, all scoped to its bridge:

```bash
iptables -I FORWARD -i ${BRIDGE} -o ${BRIDGE} -j ACCEPT              # container ↔ container
iptables -I FORWARD -i ${BRIDGE} ! -o ${BRIDGE} -j ACCEPT            # container → internet
iptables -I FORWARD -o ${BRIDGE} -m conntrack --ctstate RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT
iptables -t nat -I POSTROUTING -s ${FIXED_CIDR} ! -o ${BRIDGE} -j MASQUERADE
```

**Why not Docker-managed?** Docker's built-in iptables uses globally-named chains (`DOCKER-FORWARD`, `DOCKER-USER`, etc.). When multiple dockerd instances start, they stomp each other's rules. **Explicit iptables prevents any cross-instance interference.**

**Result:** Complete daemon-level network isolation with zero bridge-to-bridge routing (no cross-instance container connectivity at L3).

---

### File System Layout

```
${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/                    # owned by ${INSTANCE_USER}:${INSTANCE_GROUP}
├── bin/                             # Static binaries (dockerd, containerd, sysbox-*,
│                                    # docker CLI wrapper, dockyard.sh, dockyardctl symlink)
├── etc/
│   ├── daemon.json                  # Generated docker config (embedded at create time)
│   └── dockyard.env                 # Copy of config
├── lib/
│   ├── docker/                      # Docker data-root (images, containers, volumes)
│   │   └── containerd/              # Containerd content store
│   ├── sysbox/                      # sysbox-mgr data-root + sysbox-fs mountpoint
│   └── docker-config/               # DOCKER_CONFIG dir (auth, config.json)
├── log/
│   ├── dockerd.log
│   ├── containerd.log
│   ├── sysbox-mgr.log
│   └── sysbox-fs.log
└── run/                             # All runtime sockets + PIDs
    ├── docker.sock                  # API socket (root:${INSTANCE_GROUP} 660)
    ├── dockerd.pid
    ├── containerd.pid
    ├── containerd/
    │   └── containerd.sock
    └── sysbox/
        ├── sysmgr.sock
        ├── sysfs.sock
        ├── sysbox-mgr.pid
        └── sysbox-fs.pid

/etc/systemd/system/
└── ${PREFIX}docker.service          # Per-instance service (no shared sysbox service)

/etc/apparmor.d/local/fusermount3    # Per-instance AppArmor block (for Ubuntu 25.10+)
```

---

### Breaking Changes & New Features for Sandcastle

#### New in Latest Release:
1. **aarch64 support** — Dockyard now detects and handles ARM64 architectures automatically
2. **SHA256 verification** — All downloads verified on both fresh and cache hits
3. **`--keep-data` flag** — Can now preserve container data when destroying an instance
4. **dockyard-stack** — New monitoring wrapper that ensures proper daemon startup order and restart handling

#### Sysbox Version Bump:
- 0.6.7.9-tc → **0.6.7.10-tc** (includes aarch64 tarball)
- If Sandcastle pins Dockyard version, verify aarch64 compatibility on target systems

#### Config Discovery Rules (Important for Integration):
1. `$DOCKYARD_ENV` if set → use that file (error if missing)
2. `./dockyard.env` in current directory
3. `../etc/dockyard.env` relative to script (installed copy)
4. `$DOCKYARD_ROOT/etc/dockyard.env`
5. Error if none found

**Implication:** Sandcastle can pass `DOCKYARD_ENV=...` to Dockyard to use custom config paths.

#### API Socket Permissions:
- Socket is `root:${INSTANCE_GROUP} 660`
- Non-root users must join the instance group to access the socket
- Sandcastle should manage group membership for users needing access

---

### Smoke Test (What `verify` Checks)

The `verify` subcommand validates a running instance:

1. systemd service active
2. Docker socket exists
3. Docker API reachable (`docker info`)
4. Basic container run (`alpine echo`)
5. Outbound networking (`ping 1.1.1.1` from container)
6. Docker-in-Docker (`docker:26.1-dind` can start and run inner containers)

**All 6 checks must pass.** If any fails, the instance is considered broken.

---

### Critical Constraints for Sandcastle Integration

1. **Per-instance sysbox only** — Cannot share sysbox daemons across instances. Each Dockyard instance is completely isolated.

2. **Docker API socket is the only interface** — Sandcastle communicates with dockerd via `DOCKER_HOST=unix:///${DOCKER_SOCKET}`.

3. **Config must exist before most operations** — `gen-env` must be run before `create`, `enable`, `start`, etc. Only `gen-env` and `status` work without a config file (status looks for it).

4. **Service names are hardcoded at create time** — The systemd service file has all paths baked in. Changing paths after creation requires destroy + recreate.

5. **User/group ownership** — Socket is owned by `${INSTANCE_GROUP}`. Sandcastle users need to be in that group to access the socket without sudo.

6. **Network isolation is at socket/daemon level, not L3** — Containers on different instances can reach each other's bridge IPs (expected kernel routing). Isolation is achieved via separate dockerd daemons, not firewall rules between bridges.

7. **Sysbox-runc 0.6.7.9+ is required** — The upstream Nestybox sysbox does NOT support per-instance isolation (hardcoded socket paths). Must use the fork.

8. **DinD image pin** — Inner Docker containers must use `docker:26.1-dind` (or earlier), not `docker:27.x` or later, due to runc 1.3.x / procfs incompatibility with sysbox 0.6.7.

---

### Summary for Sandcastle

Dockyard is a **battle-tested, production-ready multi-daemon orchestration tool** with these key properties:

- **Single-script installation** — No external dependencies beyond bash, curl, tar, systemd
- **Fully isolated instances** — Each instance has its own dockerd, containerd, sysbox pair, bridge, user/group, and iptables rules
- **Self-contained systemd services** — Services work even if the Dockyard repository is deleted
- **No shared state** — Destroying one instance has zero effect on others
- **Complete Docker API compatibility** — Use the Docker CLI and go-docker library normally
- **Latest security hardening** — Supply chain verification, reliability fixes, AppArmor integration
- **Multi-architecture support** — x86_64 and aarch64 both supported

For Sandcastle integration, the key is: **Dockyard provides the isolated Docker environment; Sandcastle manages user access, SSH, terminal routing, and Tailscale networking** on top.

│   └── dockyard.env                 # Copy of config
├── lib/
│   ├── docker/                      # Docker data-root (images, containers, volumes)
│   │   └── containerd/              # Containerd content store
│   ├── sysbox/                      # sysbox-mgr data-root + sysbox-fs mountpoint
│   └── docker-config/               # DOCKER_CONFIG dir (auth, config.json)
├── log/
│   ├── dockerd.log
│   ├── containerd.log
│   ├── sysbox-mgr.log
│   └── sysbox-fs.log
└── run/                             # All runtime sockets + PIDs
    ├── docker.sock                  # API socket (root:${INSTANCE_GROUP} 660)
    ├── dockerd.pid
    ├── containerd.pid
    ├── containerd/
    │   └── containerd.sock
    └── sysbox/
        ├── sysmgr.sock
        ├── sysfs.sock
        ├── sysbox-mgr.pid
        └── sysbox-fs.pid

/etc/systemd/system/
└── ${PREFIX}docker.service          # Per-instance service (no shared sysbox service)

/etc/apparmor.d/local/fusermount3    # Per-instance AppArmor block (for Ubuntu 25.10+)
```

---

### Breaking Changes & New Features for Sandcastle

#### New in Latest Release:
1. **aarch64 support** — Dockyard now detects and handles ARM64 architectures automatically
2. **SHA256 verification** — All downloads verified on both fresh and cache hits
3. **`--keep-data` flag** — Can now preserve container data when destroying an instance
4. **dockyard-stack** — New monitoring wrapper that ensures proper daemon startup order and restart handling

#### Sysbox Version Bump:
- 0.6.7.9-tc → **0.6.7.10-tc** (includes aarch64 tarball)
- If Sandcastle pins Dockyard version, verify aarch64 compatibility on target systems

#### Config Discovery Rules (Important for Integration):
1. `$DOCKYARD_ENV` if set → use that file (error if missing)
2. `./dockyard.env` in current directory
3. `../etc/dockyard.env` relative to script (installed copy)
4. `$DOCKYARD_ROOT/etc/dockyard.env`
5. Error if none found

**Implication:** Sandcastle can pass `DOCKYARD_ENV=...` to Dockyard to use custom config paths.

#### API Socket Permissions:
- Socket is `root:${INSTANCE_GROUP} 660`
- Non-root users must join the instance group to access the socket
- Sandcastle should manage group membership for users needing access

---

### Smoke Test (What `verify` Checks)

The `verify` subcommand validates a running instance:

1. systemd service active
2. Docker socket exists
3. Docker API reachable (`docker info`)
4. Basic container run (`alpine echo`)
5. Outbound networking (`ping 1.1.1.1` from container)
6. Docker-in-Docker (`docker:26.1-dind` can start and run inner containers)

**All 6 checks must pass.** If any fails, the instance is considered broken.

---

### Critical Constraints for Sandcastle Integration

1. **Per-instance sysbox only** — Cannot share sysbox daemons across instances. Each Dockyard instance is completely isolated.

2. **Docker API socket is the only interface** — Sandcastle communicates with dockerd via `DOCKER_HOST=unix:///${DOCKER_SOCKET}`.

3. **Config must exist before most operations** — `gen-env` must be run before `create`, `enable`, `start`, etc. Only `gen-env` and `status` work without a config file (status looks for it).

4. **Service names are hardcoded at create time** — The systemd service file has all paths baked in. Changing paths after creation requires destroy + recreate.

5. **User/group ownership** — Socket is owned by `${INSTANCE_GROUP}`. Sandcastle users need to be in that group to access the socket without sudo.

6. **Network isolation is at socket/daemon level, not L3** — Containers on different instances can reach each other's bridge IPs (expected kernel routing). Isolation is achieved via separate dockerd daemons, not firewall rules between bridges.

7. **Sysbox-runc 0.6.7.9+ is required** — The upstream Nestybox sysbox does NOT support per-instance isolation (hardcoded socket paths). Must use the fork.

8. **DinD image pin** — Inner Docker containers must use `docker:26.1-dind` (or earlier), not `docker:27.x` or later, due to runc 1.3.x / procfs incompatibility with sysbox 0.6.7.

---

### Summary for Sandcastle

Dockyard is a **battle-tested, production-ready multi-daemon orchestration tool** with these key properties:

- **Single-script installation** — No external dependencies beyond bash, curl, tar, systemd
- **Fully isolated instances** — Each instance has its own dockerd, containerd, sysbox pair, bridge, user/group, and iptables rules
- **Self-contained systemd services** — Services work even if the Dockyard repository is deleted
- **No shared state** — Destroying one instance has zero effect on others
- **Complete Docker API compatibility** — Use the Docker CLI and go-docker library normally
- **Latest security hardening** — Supply chain verification, reliability fixes, AppArmor integration
- **Multi-architecture support** — x86_64 and aarch64 both supported

For Sandcastle integration, the key is: **Dockyard provides the isolated Docker environment; Sandcastle manages user access, SSH, terminal routing, and Tailscale networking** on top.

1→class SandboxManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  DEFAULT_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest"
     4→  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     5→
     6→  class Error < StandardError; end
     7→
     8→  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, persistent: false, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
     9→    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
    10→    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
    11→      name: name,
    12→      image: image,
    13→      status: "pending",
    14→      persistent_volume: persistent,
    15→      mount_home: mount_home,
    16→      data_path: data_path,
    17→      temporary: temporary
    18→    )
    19→
    20→    if persistent
    21→      sandbox.volume_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    22→    end
    23→
    24→    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    25→    sandbox.validate!
    26→
    27→    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    28→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    29→
    30→    # Now safe to save
    31→    sandbox.save!
    32→
    33→    # Pull image
    34→    ensure_image(image)
    35→
    36→    # Create and start container
    37→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    38→
    39→    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    40→    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
    41→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    42→    end
    43→
    44→    sandbox
    45→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    46→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    47→    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
    48→  rescue => e
    49→    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    50→    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    51→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    52→    raise Error, e.message
    53→  end
    54→
    55→  # Public method for job usage
    56→  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    57→    container = Docker::Container.create(
    58→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
    59→      "Image" => sandbox.image,
    60→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
    61→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
    62→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
    63→      "HostConfig" => {
    64→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
    65→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
    66→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
    67→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
    68→      },
    69→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
    70→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
    71→      }
    72→    )
    73→
    74→    container.start
    75→    container.refresh!
    76→    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    77→      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
    78→      raise Error, "Container failed to start: #{state_error}"
    79→    end
    80→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")
    81→
    82→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    83→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    84→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
    85→  end
    86→
    87→  # Public method for job usage
    88→  def ensure_image(image)
    89→    Docker::Image.get(image)
    90→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    91→    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
    92→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
    93→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    94→    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
    95→  end
    96→
    97→  # Public method for job usage
    98→  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    99→    # Directories bind-mounted into Sysbox containers must be world-writable
   100→    # because Sysbox maps container root to a high host UID (via /etc/subuid)
   101→    # that won't match the directory owner.
   102→
   103→    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
   104→    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
   105→
   106→    if sandbox.mount_home
   107→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   108→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   109→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, dir)
   110→    end
   111→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   112→      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   113→      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   114→
   115→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   116→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   117→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, dir)
   118→    end
   119→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   120→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(sandbox.volume_path)
   121→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, sandbox.volume_path)
   122→    end
   123→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   124→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   125→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   126→      FileUtils.mkdir_p(dir)
   127→      FileUtils.chmod(0o777, dir)
   128→    end
   129→  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   130→    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   131→  end
   132→
   133→  def destroy(sandbox:, keep_volume: false)
   134→    begin
   135→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   136→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   137→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   138→    end
   139→
   140→    begin
   141→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   142→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   143→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   144→    end
   145→
   146→    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   147→
   148→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   149→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   150→    end
   151→
   152→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   153→      begin
   154→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   155→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   156→        container.delete(force: true)
   157→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   158→        # Container already gone
   159→      end
   160→    end
   161→
   162→    unless keep_volume
   163→      FileUtils.rm_rf(sandbox.volume_path) if sandbox.volume_path.present?
   164→    end
   165→
   166→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   167→  end
   168→
   169→  def start(sandbox:)
   170→    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   171→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   172→
   173→    user = sandbox.user
   174→
   175→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   176→      begin
   177→        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   178→        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   179→        old.delete(force: true)
   180→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   181→        # already gone
   182→      end
   183→    end
   184→
   185→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   186→
   187→    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   188→    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   189→
   190→    sandbox
   191→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   192→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   193→    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
   194→  end
   195→
   196→  def stop(sandbox:)
   197→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   198→
   199→    begin
   200→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   201→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   202→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   203→    end
   204→
   205→    begin
   206→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   207→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   208→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   209→    end
   210→
   211→    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   212→
   213→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   214→      begin
   215→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   216→        container.stop(t: 10)
   217→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   218→        # already gone
   219→      end
   220→    end
   221→
   222→    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
   223→    sandbox
   224→  end
   225→
   226→  def status(sandbox:)
   227→    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   228→
   229→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   230→    info = container.json
   231→    {
   232→      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
   233→      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
   234→      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
   235→      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
   236→    }
   237→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   238→    { state: "not_found" }
   239→  end
   240→
   241→  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
   242→  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
   243→  #
   244→  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data", "workspace"
   245→  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
   246→  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
   247→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   248→
   249→    user = sandbox.user
   250→    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
   251→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data workspace]
   252→
   253→    snap = Snapshot.new(
   254→      user: user,
   255→      name: name,
   256→      label: label,
   257→      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
   258→      data_subdir: data_subdir
   259→    )
   260→
   261→    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   262→    if requested_layers.include?("container")
   263→      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   264→      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   265→      image = container.commit(
   266→        repo: repo,
   267→        tag: name,
   268→        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
   269→      )
   270→      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
   271→      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
   272→    end
   273→
   274→    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   275→    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   276→      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   277→      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
   278→      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
   279→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
   280→        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
   281→        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
   282→      end
   283→    end
   284→
   285→    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   286→    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   287→      if data_subdir.present?
   288→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   289→      else
   290→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   291→      end
   292→      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
   293→      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
   294→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
   295→        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
   296→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
   297→      end
   298→    end
   299→
   300→    # ── Workspace layer (BTRFS only) ─────────────────────────────────────────
   301→    if requested_layers.include?("workspace") && sandbox.persistent_volume? && sandbox.volume_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   302→      workspace_src  = sandbox.volume_path
   303→      workspace_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/workspace"
   304→      if Dir.exist?(workspace_src)
   305→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(workspace_src, workspace_dest)
   306→        # Store workspace alongside data_snapshot when no data layer taken
   307→        snap.data_snapshot ||= workspace_dest
   308→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(workspace_dest)
   309→      end
   310→    end
   311→
   312→    snap.save!
   313→    snap
   314→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   315→    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
   316→  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   317→    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
   318→  end
   319→
   320→  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
   321→  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
   322→    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
   323→    {
   324→      name: snap.name,
   325→      image: snap.docker_image,
   326→      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   327→      created_at: snap.created_at
   328→    }
   329→  end
   330→
   331→  def list_snapshots(user:)
   332→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   333→    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
   334→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   335→    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
   336→  end
   337→
   338→  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
   339→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   340→    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
   341→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   342→    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   343→  end
   344→
   345→  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
   346→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)
   347→
   348→    if snap
   349→      # Remove Docker image
   350→      if snap.docker_image.present?
   351→        begin
   352→          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
   353→        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   354→          # Already gone
   355→        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   356→          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
   357→        end
   358→      end
   359→
   360→      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
   361→      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
   362→        begin
   363→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
   364→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   365→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   366→        end
   367→      end
   368→
   369→      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
   370→        begin
   371→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
   372→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   373→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   374→        end
   375→      end
   376→
   377→      snap.destroy!
   378→    else
   379→      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
   380→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
   381→      begin
   382→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
   383→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   384→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   385→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   386→        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
   387→      end
   388→    end
   389→  end
   390→
   391→  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   392→    user = sandbox.user
   393→    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   394→
   395→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   396→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   397→
   398→    # Determine the Docker image to use
   399→    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   400→      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   401→    else
   402→      # Fall back to legacy naming
   403→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   404→    end
   405→
   406→    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   407→    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   408→    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   409→
   410→    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
   411→    if restore_container
   412→      begin
   413→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   414→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   415→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
   416→      end
   417→    end
   418→
   419→    begin
   420→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   421→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   422→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   423→    end
   424→
   425→    begin
   426→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   427→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   428→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   429→    end
   430→
   431→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   432→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   433→    end
   434→
   435→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   436→      begin
   437→        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   438→        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   439→        old_container.delete(force: true)
   440→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   441→        # Already gone
   442→      end
   443→    end
   444→
   445→    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   446→    if restore_home
   447→      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   448→      begin
   449→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   450→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   451→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   452→      end
   453→    end
   454→
   455→    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   456→    if restore_data
   457→      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   458→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   459→      else
   460→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   461→      end
   462→      begin
   463→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   464→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   465→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   466→      end
   467→    end
   468→
   469→    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   470→    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   471→
   472→    container = Docker::Container.create(
   473→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
   474→      "Image" => final_image,
   475→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
   476→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
   477→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
   478→      "HostConfig" => {
   479→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
   480→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
   481→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
   482→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   483→      },
   484→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
   485→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
   486→      }
   487→    )
   488→
   489→    container.start
   490→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, image: final_image, status: "running")
   491→
   492→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately after restore.
   493→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
   494→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
   495→
   496→    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   497→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   498→    end
   499→
   500→    sandbox
   501→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   502→    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   503→  end
   504→
   505→  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   506→  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   507→    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   508→
   509→    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   510→      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   511→      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   512→        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   513→        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   514→        next if tag_name.blank?
   515→        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   516→
   517→        Snapshot.create!(
   518→          user: user,
   519→          name: tag_name,
   520→          docker_image: tag,
   521→          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   522→          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   523→          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   524→        )
   525→      end
   526→    end
   527→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   528→    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   529→  end
   530→
   531→  def snapshot_json(snap)
   532→    {
   533→      name: snap.name,
   534→      label: snap.label,
   535→      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   536→      layers: snap.layers,
   537→      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   538→      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   539→      home_size: snap.home_size,
   540→      data_size: snap.data_size,
   541→      total_size: snap.total_size,
   542→      created_at: snap.created_at
   543→    }
   544→  end
   545→
   546→  def connect_info(sandbox:)
   547→    user = sandbox.user.name
   548→
   549→    unless sandbox.tailscale?
   550→      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
   551→    end
   552→
   553→    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   554→    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available — is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?
   555→
   556→    {
   557→      host: ts_ip,
   558→      port: 22,
   559→      user: user,
   560→      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
   561→      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
   562→    }
   563→  end
   564→
   565→  private
   566→
   567→  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   568→    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   569→    max_attempts.times do
   570→      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   571→
   572→      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   573→      if sandbox.status != "running"
   574→        sleep delay
   575→        next
   576→      end
   577→
   578→      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   579→      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   580→      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   581→
   582→      sleep delay
   583→    end
   584→    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   585→    nil
   586→  end
   587→
   588→  def connect_to_network(container)
   589→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   590→    network.connect(container.id)
   591→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   592→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
   593→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   594→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
   595→  end
   596→
   597→  def container_env(user, sandbox)
   598→    env = [
   599→      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
   600→      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
   601→    ]
   602→    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
   603→    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
   604→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   605→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
   606→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
   607→    env
   608→  end
   609→
   610→  def container_runtime
   611→    @container_runtime ||= begin
   612→      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
   613→      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
   614→        "sysbox-runc"
   615→      else
   616→        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
   617→        "runc"
   618→      end
   619→    end
   620→  end
   621→
   622→  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
   623→    binds = []
   624→    if sandbox.mount_home
   625→      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
   626→    end
   627→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   628→      binds << "#{sandbox.volume_path}:/workspace"
   629→    end
   630→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   631→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   632→      binds << "#{host_path}:/data"
   633→    end
   634→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
   635→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   636→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   637→      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
   638→    end
   639→    binds
   640→  end
   641→end

     2→  DATA_DIR     = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")

    10→  # Opens a web terminal for the given sandbox.
    11→  # type: "tmux" (default) or "shell"
    12→  # Writes Traefik config (idempotent) and returns the terminal URL.
    13→  def open(sandbox:, type: "tmux")
    14→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    15→
    16→    write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    17→    terminal_url(sandbox, type)
    18→  end
    19→
    20→  # Pre-writes the Traefik config without requiring the sandbox to be open.
    21→  # Called at container start so routes are ready immediately.
    22→  def prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    23→    write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    24→  end
    25→
    26→  # Deletes the Traefik config for the given sandbox.
    27→  def close(sandbox:)
    28→    delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
    29→  end

    31→  # Scans terminal-*.yml files; removes configs for sandboxes that no longer exist or aren't running.
    32→  def cleanup_orphaned
    33→    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-*.yml")).each do |path|
    34→      match = File.basename(path).match(/\Aterminal-(\d+)\.yml\z/)
    35→      next unless match
    36→
    37→      sandbox_id = match[1].to_i
    38→      sandbox = Sandbox.find_by(id: sandbox_id)
    39→
    40→      if sandbox.nil? || sandbox.status != "running"
    41→        File.delete(path)
    42→        Rails.logger.info("TerminalManager: removed orphaned Traefik config #{File.basename(path)}")
    43→      end
    44→    end
    45→  end
    46→
    47→  private
    48→
    49→  def terminal_url(sandbox, type)
    50→    "/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/#{type}"
    51→  end
    52→
    53→  def write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    54→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
    55→    config_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
    56→    return false if File.exist?(config_path)
    57→
    58→    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    59→    id   = sandbox.id
    60→    container = sandbox.full_name
    61→
    62→    if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
    63→      tmux_rule  = "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/tmux`)"
    64→      shell_rule = "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/shell`)"
    65→    else
    66→      tmux_rule  = "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/tmux`)"
    67→      shell_rule = "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/shell`)"
    68→    end
    69→
    70→    config = {
    71→      "http" => {
    72→        "routers" => {
    73→          "terminal-#{id}-tmux" => {
    74→            "rule"        => tmux_rule,
    75→            "service"     => "terminal-#{id}-tmux",
    76→            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
    77→            "tls"         => tls_config,
    78→            "middlewares" => [ "terminal-auth-#{id}", "terminal-#{id}-strip-tmux" ],
    79→            "priority"    => 100
    80→          },
    81→          "terminal-#{id}-shell" => {
    82→            "rule"        => shell_rule,
    83→            "service"     => "terminal-#{id}-shell",
    84→            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
    85→            "tls"         => tls_config,
    86→            "middlewares" => [ "terminal-auth-#{id}", "terminal-#{id}-strip-shell" ],
    87→            "priority"    => 100
    88→          }
    89→        },
    90→        "middlewares" => {
    91→          "terminal-auth-#{id}" => {
    92→            "forwardAuth" => {
    93→              "address"            => "http://sandcastle-web:80/terminal/auth",
    94→              "trustForwardHeader" => true
    95→            }
    96→          },
    97→          "terminal-#{id}-strip-tmux" => {
    98→            "stripPrefix" => { "prefixes" => [ "/terminal/#{id}/tmux" ] }
    99→          },
   100→          "terminal-#{id}-strip-shell" => {
   101→            "stripPrefix" => { "prefixes" => [ "/terminal/#{id}/shell" ] }
   102→          }
   103→        },
   104→        "services" => {
   105→          "terminal-#{id}-tmux" => {
   106→            "loadBalancer" => {
   107→              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{container}:#{TMUX_PORT}" } ]
   108→            }
   109→          },
   110→          "terminal-#{id}-shell" => {
   111→            "loadBalancer" => {
   112→              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{container}:#{SHELL_PORT}" } ]
   113→            }
   114→          }
   115→        }
   116→      }
   117→    }
   118→
   119→    File.write(config_path, config.to_yaml)
   120→    true
   121→  end
   122→
   123→  def tls_config
   124→    case ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"]
   125→    when "selfsigned", "mkcert"
   126→      {}
   127→    else
   128→      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
   129→    end
   130→  end
   131→
   132→  def delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
   133→    path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
   134→    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   135→  end
   136→end

1→class TailscaleManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  TAILSCALE_IMAGE = "tailscale/tailscale:latest"
     4→  TAILSCALE_TAG = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TAILSCALE_TAG", "").presence
     5→  LOGIN_URL_PATTERN = %r{https://login\.tailscale\.com/\S+}
     6→
     7→  class Error < StandardError; end
     8→
     9→  # Restore sidecar from persisted tailscaled.state without re-authentication.
    10→  # Used after reinstall or crash when interactive-login state survives on disk.
    11→  # Note: we intentionally skip checking File.exist? for the state file because
    12→  # the tailscale directory is root-owned (drwx------) and unreadable by the
    13→  # sandcastle process. Containerboot will handle missing state gracefully.
    14→  def restore_from_state(user:)
    15→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    16→
    17→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    18→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    19→    subnet = subnet_for(user)
    20→
    21→    pull_image
    22→    create_network(network_name, subnet)
    23→    remove_existing_container(container_name)
    24→    # Intentionally skip clear_tailscale_state — we rely on existing saved credentials
    25→    # Use raw tailscaled (same as interactive flow) — NOT containerboot.
    26→    # Containerboot runs `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, which would expire the
    27→    # saved node key. Raw tailscaled exits cleanly without touching auth state.
    28→    container = create_sidecar(
    29→      name: container_name,
    30→      user: user,
    31→      network: network_name,
    32→      subnet: subnet,
    33→      auth_key: nil
    34→    )
    35→    container.start
    36→
    37→    user.update!(
    38→      tailscale_state: "enabled",
    39→      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
    40→      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
    41→      tailscale_network: network_name
    42→    )
    43→    user
    44→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    45→    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    46→    raise Error, "Failed to restore Tailscale from state: #{e.message}"
    47→  end

    49→  # Legacy: one-shot enable with an auth key (still supported for automation)
    50→  def enable(user:, auth_key:)
    51→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    52→
    53→    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: auth_key)
    54→
    55→    user.update!(
    56→      tailscale_state: "enabled",
    57→      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
    58→      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
    59→      tailscale_network: network_name
    60→    )
    61→    save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
    62→    user
    63→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    64→    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    65→    raise Error, "Failed to enable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
    66→  end

    68→  # Phase 1: create and start sidecar, return immediately
    69→  def start_login(user:)
    70→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled?
    71→
    72→    # If pending, clean up the old attempt first
    73→    cleanup_sidecar(user) if user.tailscale_pending?
    74→
    75→    hostname = Rails.cache.read("ts_hostname:#{user.id}")
    76→    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: nil, hostname: hostname)
    77→
    78→    user.update!(
    79→      tailscale_state: "pending",
    80→      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
    81→      tailscale_network: network_name
    82→    )
    83→
    84→    { status: "starting" }
    85→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    86→    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    87→    raise Error, "Failed to start Tailscale login: #{e.message}"
    88→  end
    89→
    90→  # Phase 2: progressive login status check
    91→  # Returns: { status: "starting" | "waiting_for_url" | "login_ready" | "authenticated" | "error" }
    92→  def check_login(user:)
    93→    raise Error, "No pending login" unless user.tailscale_pending?
    94→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?
    95→
    96→    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
    97→    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    98→    return { status: "starting", message: "Starting sidecar container..." } unless running
    99→
   100→    # Kick off `tailscale up` in the background if not already done
   101→    cache_key = "ts_login_started:#{user.id}"
   102→    unless Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
   103→      subnet = subnet_for(user)
   104→      tag = Rails.cache.read("ts_tag:#{user.id}") || TAILSCALE_TAG
   105→      tag_flag = tag ? " --advertise-tags=#{tag}" : ""
   106→      container.exec([
   107→        "sh", "-c",
   108→        "tailscale up --reset" \
   109→        " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
   110→        "#{tag_flag}" \
   111→        " --hostname=#{container.json.dig("Config", "Hostname")}" \
   112→        " --timeout=120s &"
   113→      ])
   114→      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, true, expires_in: 5.minutes)
   115→      return { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   116→    end
   117→
   118→    # Check tailscale status for auth progress
   119→    status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
   120→    if status_out.first.any?
   121→      ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
   122→      case ts_status["BackendState"]
   123→      when "Running"
   124→        Rails.cache.delete(cache_key)
   125→        user.update!(tailscale_state: "enabled", tailscale_auto_connect: true)
   126→        ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
   127→        tailscale_ip = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
   128→        return {
   129→          status: "authenticated",
   130→          tailscale_ip: tailscale_ip,
   131→          hostname: ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName"),
   132→          tailnet: ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
   133→        }
   134→      when "NeedsLogin"
   135→        auth_url = ts_status["AuthURL"]
   136→        if auth_url.present?
   137→          return { status: "login_ready", login_url: auth_url, message: "Click the link to authenticate" }
   138→        end
   139→      end
   140→    end
   141→
   142→    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   143→  rescue JSON::ParserError
   144→    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   145→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   146→    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
   147→    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
   148→    raise Error, "Sidecar container disappeared"
   149→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   150→    raise Error, "Failed to check login: #{e.message}"
   151→  end

   153→  def disable(user:)
   154→    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" if user.tailscale_disabled?
   155→
   156→    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
   157→
   158→    # Disconnect all sandboxes first
   159→    user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true).find_each do |sandbox|
   160→      disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   161→    end
   162→
   163→    cleanup_sidecar(user)
   164→
   165→    user.update!(
   166→      tailscale_state: "disabled",
   167→      tailscale_container_id: nil,
   168→      tailscale_network: nil
   169→    )
   170→    delete_auth_key(user)
   171→    user
   172→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   173→    raise Error, "Failed to disable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   174→  end
   175→
   176→  def status(user:)
   177→    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" unless user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
   178→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?
   179→
   180→    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
   181→    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
   182→
   183→    ts_sandboxes = user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true)
   184→    sandbox_ips = ts_sandboxes.map do |sb|
   185→      ip = sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sb)
   186→      { name: sb.name, ip: ip }
   187→    end
   188→
   189→    result = {
   190→      running: running,
   191→      container_id: user.tailscale_container_id[0..11],
   192→      network: user.tailscale_network,
   193→      connected_sandboxes: ts_sandboxes.count,
   194→      sandboxes: sandbox_ips
   195→    }
   196→
   197→    if running
   198→      ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
   199→      result[:tailscale_ip] = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
   200→
   201→      status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
   202→      if status_out.first.any?
   203→        begin
   204→          ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
   205→          result[:hostname] = ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName")
   206→          result[:tailnet] = ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
   207→          result[:online] = ts_status.dig("Self", "Online")
   208→          tags = ts_status.dig("Self", "Tags")
   209→          result[:tags] = tags if tags.present?
   210→        rescue JSON::ParserError
   211→          # Status not available yet
   212→        end
   213→      end
   214→    else
   215→      # Grab last 20 lines of container logs for debugging
   216→      logs = container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, tail: 20)
   217→      result[:logs] = logs.encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace).strip if logs.present?
   218→      result[:exit_code] = container.json.dig("State", "ExitCode")
   219→      result[:error_reason] = container.json.dig("State", "Error")
   220→    end
   221→
   222→    result
   223→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   224→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found — try disabling and re-enabling Tailscale"
   225→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   226→    raise Error, "Failed to get Tailscale status: #{e.message}"
   227→  end
   228→
   229→  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   230→    user = sandbox.user
   231→    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
   232→    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   233→
   234→    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   235→    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
   236→    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
   237→    sandbox
   238→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   239→    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   240→  end
   241→
   242→  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   243→    user = sandbox.user
   244→    return sandbox unless sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   245→
   246→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   247→      begin
   248→        network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   249→        network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
   250→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
   251→        # Network/container already gone or container not connected — desired state reached
   252→      end
   253→    end
   254→
   255→    sandbox.update!(tailscale: false)
   256→    sandbox
   257→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   258→    raise Error, "Failed to disconnect sandbox from Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   259→  end
   260→
   261→  def sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox:)
   262→    user = sandbox.user
   263→    return nil unless sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.container_id.present? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   264→
   265→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   266→    container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", user.tailscale_network, "IPAddress")
   267→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   268→    nil
   269→  end
   270→
   271→  def auth_key_path(user)
   272→    File.join(DATA_DIR, "users", user.name, "tailscale", ".auth_key")
   273→  end
   274→
   275→  private
   276→
   277→  def save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
   278→    path = auth_key_path(user)
   279→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(path))
   280→    File.write(path, auth_key)
   281→    File.chmod(0o600, path)
   282→  end
   283→
   284→  def delete_auth_key(user)
   285→    path = auth_key_path(user)
   286→    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   287→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   288→    # already gone
   289→  end

   291→  def create_and_start_sidecar(user:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
   292→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   293→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   294→    subnet = subnet_for(user)
   295→
   296→    pull_image
   297→    create_network(network_name, subnet)
   298→    remove_existing_container(container_name)
   299→    clear_tailscale_state(user)
   300→    container = create_sidecar(
   301→      name: container_name,
   302→      user: user,
   303→      network: network_name,
   304→      subnet: subnet,
   305→      auth_key: auth_key,
   306→      hostname: hostname
   307→    )
   308→    container.start
   309→
   310→    [ network_name, container ]
   311→  end
   312→
   313→  def remove_existing_container(name)
   314→    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
   315→    container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   316→    container.delete(force: true)
   317→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   318→    # No existing container
   319→  end
   320→
   321→  # Remove stale tailscaled.state so the daemon starts fresh.
   322→  # Stale state causes tailscaled to immediately set up VPN routes before
   323→  # authentication, which silently drops all normal internet traffic.
   324→  def clear_tailscale_state(user)
   325→    state_file = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale/tailscaled.state"
   326→    File.delete(state_file) if File.exist?(state_file)
   327→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   328→    # already gone
   329→  end
   330→
   331→  def cleanup_sidecar(user)
   332→    # Try by stored container ID first, then by name as fallback
   333→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   334→    [user.tailscale_container_id, container_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   335→      begin
   336→        container = Docker::Container.get(ref)
   337→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   338→        container.delete(force: true)
   339→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   340→        # Already gone
   341→      end
   342→    end
   343→
   344→    # Clean up network by stored name, then by convention
   345→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   346→    [user.tailscale_network, network_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   347→      begin
   348→        Docker::Network.get(ref).delete
   349→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   350→        # Already gone
   351→      end
   352→    end
   353→  end
   354→
   355→  def cleanup_on_failure(user)
   356→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   357→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   358→    begin
   359→      Docker::Container.get(container_name).delete(force: true)
   360→    rescue StandardError
   361→      nil
   362→    end
   363→    begin
   364→      Docker::Network.get(network_name).delete
   365→    rescue StandardError
   366→      nil
   367→    end
   368→    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
   369→  end
   370→
   371→  def pull_image
   372→    Docker::Image.get(TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   373→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   374→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   375→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   376→    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{TAILSCALE_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
   377→  end
   378→
   379→  def subnet_for(user)
   380→    # If the network already exists, read its actual subnet (stable across restarts)
   381→    begin
   382→      network = Docker::Network.get("sc-ts-net-#{user.name}")
   383→      ipam = network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config")
   384→      return ipam.first["Subnet"] if ipam&.first
   385→    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   386→      # Network doesn't exist yet — fall through to generate a random /24
   387→    end
   388→
   389→    base = ENV["DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"]
   390→    if base
   391→      parts = base.split("/").first.split(".").map(&:to_i)
   392→    else
   393→      parts = [ 10, rand(1..254), 0, 0 ]
   394→    end
   395→    parts[2] = rand(1..254)
   396→    "#{parts[0]}.#{parts[1]}.#{parts[2]}.0/24"
   397→  end
   398→
   399→  def create_network(name, subnet)
   400→    Docker::Network.get(name)
   401→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   402→    Docker::Network.create(
   403→      name,
   404→      "Driver" => "bridge",
   405→      "IPAM" => {
   406→        "Config" => [ { "Subnet" => subnet } ]
   407→      }
   408→    )
   409→  end
   410→
   411→  def create_sidecar(name:, user:, network:, subnet:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
   412→    state_dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale"
   413→    ts_hostname = hostname.presence || "sc-#{user.name}"
   414→
   415→    config = {
   416→      "Image" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE,
   417→      "name" => name,
   418→      "Hostname" => ts_hostname,
   419→      "HostConfig" => {
   420→        "NetworkMode" => network,
   421→        "CapAdd" => [ "NET_ADMIN", "SYS_MODULE" ],
   422→        "Devices" => [
   423→          { "PathOnHost" => "/dev/net/tun", "PathInContainer" => "/dev/net/tun", "CgroupPermissions" => "rwm" }
   424→        ],
   425→        "Sysctls" => { "net.ipv4.ip_forward" => "1" },
   426→        "Binds" => [ "#{state_dir}:/var/lib/tailscale" ],
   427→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   428→      }
   429→    }
   430→
   431→    if auth_key.present?
   432→      # Use containerboot (default entrypoint) with auth key for automated flow
   433→      config["Env"] = [
   434→        "TS_STATE_DIR=/var/lib/tailscale",
   435→        "TS_HOSTNAME=#{ts_hostname}",
   436→        "TS_EXTRA_ARGS=--advertise-routes=#{subnet} --accept-routes#{TAILSCALE_TAG ? " --advertise-tags=#{TAILSCALE_TAG}" : ""}",
   437→        "TS_AUTH_ONCE=true",
   438→        "TS_AUTHKEY=#{auth_key}"
   439→      ]
   440→    else
   441→      # Run tailscaled directly — we manage login via `tailscale up`.
   442→      # Also used for restore_from_state: raw tailscaled exits without running
   443→      # `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, preserving saved credentials in state file.
   444→      config["Entrypoint"] = [ "tailscaled", "--state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state" ]
   445→      config["Cmd"] = []
   446→    end
   447→
   448→    Docker::Container.create(config)
   449→  end
   450→end
   451→

1→class TerminalController < ApplicationController
     2→  allow_unauthenticated_access only: :auth
     3→  skip_before_action :require_password_change, only: :auth
     4→  skip_forgery_protection only: :open
     5→
     6→  def open
     7→    sandbox = find_sandbox
     8→    type    = params[:type].presence_in(%w[tmux shell]) || "tmux"
     9→    url     = TerminalManager.new.open(sandbox: sandbox, type: type)
    10→    redirect_to terminal_redirect_url(url), allow_other_host: true, status: :see_other
    11→  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    12→    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
    13→  end
    14→
    15→  def close
    16→    sandbox = find_sandbox
    17→
    18→    TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
    19→    redirect_to root_path, notice: "Terminal closed"
    20→  rescue TerminalManager::Error => e
    21→    redirect_to root_path, alert: e.message
    22→  end
    23→
    24→  private
    25→
    26→  def find_sandbox
    27→    Current.user.sandboxes.active.find(params[:id])
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  # Build the full terminal URL. In production, Traefik is the entry point
    31→  # so a relative path works. In local dev (selfsigned TLS), Rails may be
    32→  # accessed directly on a different port, so we need an absolute URL.
    33→  def terminal_redirect_url(path)
    34→    base = ENV["SANDCASTLE_TERMINAL_URL"]
    35→    base ? "#{base}#{path}" : path
    36→  end
    37→
    38→  public
    39→
    40→  # Called by Traefik forwardAuth. Returns 200 to allow, or redirects to
    41→  # login (which Traefik passes through to the browser).
    42→  def auth
    43→    forwarded_uri = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Uri"] || ""
    44→    match = forwarded_uri.match(%r{/terminal/(\d+)/(tmux|shell)})
    45→    head(:unauthorized) and return unless match
    46→
    47→    session_record = find_session_by_cookie
    48→    unless session_record
    49→      proto = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Proto"] || "https"
    50→      host  = request.headers["X-Forwarded-Host"] || request.host_with_port
    51→      original_url = "#{proto}://#{host}#{forwarded_uri}"
    52→      session[:return_to_after_authenticating] = original_url
    53→      redirect_to new_session_url, allow_other_host: true
    54→      return
    55→    end
    56→
    57→    user    = session_record.user
    58→    sandbox = Sandbox.active.find_by(id: match[1].to_i)
    59→    head(:unauthorized) and return unless sandbox && (sandbox.user_id == user.id || user.admin?)
    60→
    61→    head :ok
    62→  end
    63→end
    64→

Usage: installer.sh [COMMAND]

Sandcastle installer — sets up Docker (via Dockyard), Traefik, and the
Sandcastle platform.

Commands:
  gen-env      Generate sandcastle.env config file (default: ./sandcastle.env)
  install      Install or upgrade Sandcastle (default)
  update       Pull latest images and restart services
  reset        Tear down existing install, then reinstall
  uninstall    Remove Sandcastle completely
  help         Show this help message

Environment:
  SANDCASTLE_ENV   Path to config file (default search order:
                   ./sandcastle.env → <script_dir>/sandcastle.env →
                   $SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env)

Workflow:
  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config
  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # edit to taste
  3. sudo installer.sh install         # install (finds ./sandcastle.env)
USAGE
  exit 0
fi

cmd_gen_env() {
  local out="${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-./sandcastle.env}"
  [ -f "$out" ] && die "File already exists: $out"

  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
  local app_image="${APP_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest}"
  local sandbox_image="${SANDBOX_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest}"
  local user="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandcastle}"
  local group="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP:-$user}"
  local uid="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"
  local gid="${SANDCASTLE_GID:-220568}"
  local http_port="${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT:-80}"
  local https_port="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}"
  local tls_mode="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-selfsigned}"

  # Auto-detect host IP
  local host="${SANDCASTLE_HOST:-}"
  if [ -z "$host" ]; then
    host=$(hostname -I 2>/dev/null | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -E '^[0-9]+\.' | head -1 || true)

install_mkcert() {
  if command -v mkcert &>/dev/null; then
    return 0
  fi
  info "Installing mkcert..."
  local arch
  arch=$(uname -m)
  local mkcert_arch
  case "$arch" in
    x86_64)  mkcert_arch="amd64" ;;
    aarch64) mkcert_arch="arm64" ;;
    *)       die "Unsupported architecture for mkcert: $arch" ;;
  esac
  curl -fsSL "https://dl.filippo.io/mkcert/latest?for=linux/${mkcert_arch}" \
    -o /usr/local/bin/mkcert
  chmod +x /usr/local/bin/mkcert
  ok "mkcert installed"
}

# Write the bundled dockyard.sh to /tmp so it can be invoked without wget.
write_dockyard_sh() {
  cat > /tmp/dockyard.sh <<'__DOCKYARD_BUNDLED_EOF__'
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"

# ── Env loading ──────────────────────────────────────────────

LOADED_ENV_FILE=""

# Returns 0 on success, 1 if no config file exists.
# Exits immediately if DOCKYARD_ENV is set but the file is missing.
try_load_env() {
    local script_env="${SCRIPT_DIR}/../etc/dockyard.env"
    local root_env="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-/dockyard}/docker-runtime/etc/dockyard.env"

    if [ -n "${DOCKYARD_ENV:-}" ]; then
        if [ ! -f "$DOCKYARD_ENV" ]; then
            echo "Error: DOCKYARD_ENV file not found: ${DOCKYARD_ENV}" >&2
            exit 1
        fi
        LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$DOCKYARD_ENV")" && pwd)/$(basename "$DOCKYARD_ENV")"
    elif [ -f "./dockyard.env" ]; then
        LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(pwd)/dockyard.env"
    elif [ -f "$script_env" ]; then
        LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$script_env")" && pwd)/$(basename "$script_env")"
    elif [ -f "$root_env" ]; then
        LOADED_ENV_FILE="$root_env"
    else
        return 1
    fi

    echo "Loading ${LOADED_ENV_FILE}..."
    set -a; source "$LOADED_ENV_FILE"; set +a
}

load_env() {
    if ! try_load_env; then
        echo "Error: No config found." >&2
        echo "Run './dockyard.sh gen-env' to generate one, or set DOCKYARD_ENV." >&2
        exit 1
    fi
}

derive_vars() {
    DOCKYARD_ROOT="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-/dockyard}"
    DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-dy_}"
    DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-172.30.0.1/24}"
    DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-172.30.0.0/24}"
    DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-172.31.0.0/16}"
    DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"

    RUNTIME_DIR="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker-runtime"
    BIN_DIR="${RUNTIME_DIR}/bin"
    ETC_DIR="${RUNTIME_DIR}/etc"
    LOG_DIR="${RUNTIME_DIR}/log"
    RUN_DIR="${RUNTIME_DIR}/run"
    BRIDGE="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}docker0"
    EXEC_ROOT="/run/${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}docker"
    SERVICE_NAME="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}docker"
    DOCKER_SOCKET="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker.sock"
    CONTAINERD_SOCKET="${EXEC_ROOT}/containerd/containerd.sock"
    DOCKER_DATA="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/docker"
    SYSBOX_SERVICE_NAME="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX}sysbox"
}

# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
